report_name: UN World Food Programme Breach Exposes Data of 600,000 Gaza Households

threat_actors.name: Unknown Actor
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actor responsible for the May 2026 data breach of the UN World Food Programme's aid registration system for Palestine.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor gained unauthorized access to the WFP's public-facing self-registration application for Palestine. Hunting hints suggest this could have been via SQL Injection, XSS, or IDOR.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor likely used automated methods to exfiltrate the data of approximately 600,000 households. Hunting hints in the report suggest looking for a large number of requests from a single IP to enumerate user data.

identities.name: United Nations World Food Programme
identities.description: The humanitarian aid organization whose self-registration application for Palestine was breached, exposing the data of 600,000 households.

identities.name: Palestinian aid recipients in Gaza
identities.description: Approximately 600,000 households in Gaza whose personal data (names, ID numbers, phone numbers, location) was exposed in the breach of the WFP aid registration system.

indicators.name: Potential SQL Injection Attempt in URI
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential SQL Injection Attempt in URI
id: 2c1b4d5a-9e8f-4a7b-8c6d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects common SQL injection patterns in the URI, as suggested by hunting hints in the WFP data breach report.
references:
  - https://www.scworld.com/brief/world-food-programme-reports-data-breach-affecting-palestinian-beneficiaries
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-09
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - "' or 1=1"
      - "' or '1'='1"
      - "'; exec"
      - " union select "
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Code snippets in URLs.
indicators.description: Detects common SQL injection patterns in the URI, as suggested by hunting hints in the WFP data breach report.

indicators.name: Potential Web Application User Enumeration via URL Parameters
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Web Application User Enumeration via URL Parameters
id: 3d2c5e6b-8f7e-4b8c-9d7e-2f3a4b5c6d7e
status: experimental
description: Detects URL patterns that may indicate Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) or user enumeration attempts, as suggested by hunting hints in the WFP data breach report.
references:
  - https://www.scworld.com/brief/world-food-programme-reports-data-breach-affecting-palestinian-beneficiaries
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-09
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1119
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-query|re: '(?i)(user|account|customer|member)_?(id|no|number|num)=\d+'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate application traffic that uses these common parameter names. This rule is intended for hunting and requires correlation with request volume or other anomalies.
indicators.description: Detects URL patterns that may indicate Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) or user enumeration attempts, as suggested by hunting hints in the WFP data breach report.

relationships.source_name: Potential SQL Injection Attempt in URI
